Patch Set 1:

There are a number of patches being backported to b2_12 that appear to be mostly cosmetic.  Are this being ported because it is critical for later patches that have actual fixes?  I don't think we should be backporting cleanup patches, only those that are important for fixing bugs (either directly or are absolutely required for later patches that are fixing a bug).

I'd think some minor context differences in backported fixes is OK to work around when porting a patch.  We already have a huge backlog of patches pending for b2_12, so we should IMHO limit backports to actually-required fixes.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Since this is a backport, I don't think we should be changing the patch.  However, I wanted to point out that removing "-o" and "-a" doesn't mean we need to have multiple test conditions.  The || and && operators can be used inside [[...]] along with (...) for precedence instead of doing bash-level grouping.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 4cf34cbf68eed1fd201f0eb4bd9f3155e805cef9 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-12895 mdt: check if object exists first

commit.subject: LU-11673 tests: replace obsolete '-o' to '||'
commit.message:
LU-11673 tests: replace obsolete '-o' to '||'

Since use of -o and -a are marked as obsolete in shell
test ([), we need to switch from using [ expr1 –o expr2 ]
to [ expr1] || [ expr2 ].

Make this change for sanity tests.

This is a partial back port of:
Lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/33670
Lustre-commit: 6d277f126df7605d402255333180b0ca03991613

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <jnunez@whamcloud.com>

Reviewed-by: Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Minh Diep <mdiep@whamcloud.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
